description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by two wide, dark metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood displays a natural grain pattern with knots and subtle color variations, giving it a rustic, weathered appearance. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made from the same wood as the staves, with the top surface showing clear plank divisions. One side of the barrel features a small, round wooden bung, suggesting it can be used for storing or dispensing liquids. The metal hoops show slight wear and patina, indicating age and outdoor exposure. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, courtyards, or near storage areas, and is suitable for holding liquids or dry goods.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with dark metal hoops and a visible wooden bung, featuring natural wood grain and a rustic finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the barrel's wooden staves, metal hoops, and a clear view of the top surface with wood grain.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the barrel and the metal hoops, with no bung visible.
description_view_2: Another side view similar to view 1, with subtle wood grain and metal hoop details, no bung visible.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the wooden bung on the lower part of the barrel, along with the wood grain and metal hoops.
